tokens: And we bypass Station 7 and we go to 8.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: All right we figure a heading of 274 and 0.8.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: ...014 and 2.9 and they want a ray.
(Responding to Tony) Okay, so that's distance, right?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, John, I make it six point...(correcting himself) 7.5 on distance (driven).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Look at that blue rock that we just whacked on!
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And when you get there, you'll read 3.0 (range) and 10 degrees (bearing).
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) Okay, we're going downslope...(correcting himself) cross-slope, Tony.
And I feel like I'm about to fall out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're still in the blocky field.
In fact, it's just South Ray material, I think, that's all over the place.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I can still see old Orion!
We sure parked on a...You landed on the highest point around, John!
Even down in that crater you can still see it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Say, not bad for a Navy pilot.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: No; he did a great job.
Okay, we're back into a thicker part of the ray, Tony.
The regolith is here covered with cobbles (about 64 mm to 256 mm) about 40 to 50 percent.
You're gonna have to bear way left, John.
(Pause) Okay, now, if we hold that range, like, tack in.
Look at that piece of glass we just rolled over.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Gee, we sure hope you don't get a flat tire there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (To Tony) Man, if you could see these rocks, you'd hope it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, and I'm not so sure (we didn't hit it with the bottom of the Rover)...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Right; and there should be a lot of boulders in here, and we'd like you to get a lot of the Hasselblad photos.
And then when you go past the north side of
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, we're not going to be able to see Stubby.
It's on the other side of a big ridge.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're down in a little swale now, and there's about a 30-meter (tall) ridge off to our left about 300 meters; it blocks out Stubby.
You want us to go up and travel along that ridge?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'd like to see back into Stubby.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, why don't you press on up there?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We've got to go by that big rock.
Is that where we're going, to that big rock?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That'd be a great place to sample the ray.
But they want to go left, about 10 o'clock, up onto that top of that ridge.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay; will you be able to take pictures?
(because of the dust on his lens)?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This is really a...really a ray.
In fact, you can see coming out over the ridge, you can distinctly see the
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And the contact I'm talking about is at 12 o'clock, probably a couple of hundred meters (ahead).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You know, I don't want to be discouraged about this sort of thing, but I feel like this may be a problem we're going to have to attack statistically out here.
Because, boy, it is really difficult to tell, just looking at a rock; except for the roundness, it's difficult to tell what kind of a rock it is.
Now there's a vesicular-looking rock right there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That's the first rock I've seen with vesicles in it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, Tony, we're traveling, now, southwest.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And we've still got a couple hundred meters to go up to the ridge.
John, why don't you swing directly south and let's just go straight up that beauty, see what we see up there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're making six tenths...(correcting himself) or six kilometers an hour (because of the slope).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Have you got full throttle on?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, you want to read some amps?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, what it is, is we've lost the rear-wheel drive.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Not reading any amps on the rear wheel.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And with that in mind, I'd just as soon not go up to this ridge, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay; we suggest you head on towards 8 and stay on fairly flat ground, and we'll work up a procedure.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: John, why don't we check it?
Can you turn the forward (power) off and see if we move?
(Pause) It just might be a steep slope.
But the front wheels were really digging in.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony, at 007/3.1 - passing another secondary that's elongate in the direction of South Ray and it...Those are the big blocks they were talking about, John.
Right over there, there are four or five of them.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And how fast are you making now, John?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The regolith is still...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: He wants to (know) how fast we're going.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: How about stopping up there in the middle of all of those big boulders, John?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're about...(Long Pause) There's one that's overturnable, I'll bet you.
(Pause) Look at that elongate one, that shred there.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Tony, we're at 00...(correcting himself) 010 at 3.0, and we've got about three or four 2- or 3-meter-size blocks, one black and some white ones.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (From memory) Okay; 180, John, on the heading.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, and we're looking primarily for blocks from South Ray.
So if you feel like this is the ray, this sounds great.
(Pause) And we don't need a Nav update, so 180 is great.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I definitely think...This is fine here.
Get on up a little bit over...That full throttle?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: My best guess of what happened (to the drive power)...of what may have happened, Houston, is that cut a wire or something on the back.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, a wire going back there to that aft thing.
On the way down here - the regolith and everything being what it is - when we were bouncing up in the air, we came down on at least two rocks that I know about.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, it's a sort of dynamic (chuckles) situation that I don't think anybody was thinking about much.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Unintentional transmission by the Flight Director) Can he see those wires?
transcript.speaker: Frank

tokens: Starting our pan from about the 1 o'clock position of the Rover, sort of bracketing these blocks here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: John, before I do this (pan), how about checking my (Hasselblad) lens?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: How's mine (meaning his Hasselblad lens), Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Man, those battery covers are filthy.
transcript.speaker: Duke
